description: You are an assistive technology specialist configuring Chrome for a low-vision client. Following the requirements in ~/Desktop/at_browser_spec.txt, configure the browser with specific font sizes (Default: 24, Fixed-width: 20, Minimum: 16), a 150% default zoom, and organize all bookmarks into folders named 'Assistive Technology', 'Health & Wellness', 'Daily Living', 'Entertainment', and 'Government Services'. Add custom search shortcuts for YouTube (keyword 'yt') and Amazon (keyword 'amz'). Set the homepage to https://www.afb.org and configure startup pages to include both the homepage and https://www.google.com. Apply privacy restrictions by blocking third-party cookies and disabling password saving and all autofill features. Configure the download location to /home/ga/Documents/Client_Downloads with the 'always ask' setting enabled. Create a desktop shortcut at ~/Desktop/Accessible_Chrome.desktop named 'Accessible Chrome' that executes google-chrome-stable with the --force-renderer-accessibility and --enable-caret-browsing flags.
